description: An Energy Analyst at a utility company is exploring ways to reduce the variability of renewable energy generation on their grid. Model a 50 MW PV plant (50,000 kW DC, tilt 20 degrees, azimuth 180 degrees) using a bundled TMY solar resource and a 50 MW Wind plant (25 turbines of 2000 kW each, hub height 80m, rotor diameter 100m) using a bundled .srw wind resource. Extract the hourly AC power profiles and calculate the Coefficient of Variation (CV = standard deviation / mean) for three 100 MW scenarios: 100 MW PV (2x 50 MW profile), 100 MW Wind (2x 50 MW profile), and 100 MW Hybrid (sum of 50 MW PV and 50 MW Wind profiles). Save the metrics to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/hybrid_complementarity.json using the keys: 'pv_annual_energy_kwh', 'wind_annual_energy_kwh', 'mean_50mw_pv_kw', 'mean_50mw_wind_kw', 'mean_100mw_hybrid_kw', 'cv_100mw_pv', 'cv_100mw_wind', and 'cv_100mw_hybrid'. Save the analysis script to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/hybrid_analysis.py or the SAM project to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/hybrid_analysis.sam.
